title: Hybrid Analytical-Neural Modification of the Heun Method for Parametric Duffing Equations
authors: Tatyana A. Shemyakina, Dmitry A. Tarkhov, Igor Y. Vandakurov
abstract: A hybrid analytical-neural approach for solving parametric ordinary differential equations based on a modified Heun scheme is presented. The method combines the analytical modification of classical numerical integration with neural network parametrisation of scheme coefficients, enabling the construction of compact parametric surrogate models. Instead of directly approximating the solution, a neural network is employed to adaptively adjust the numerical scheme parameters. The approach is validated using the Duffing equation as a benchmark nonlinear system. Several configurations are investigated, including scalar optimisation and neural parametrisation of the scheme parameter as functions of one and two variables. Numerical experiments demonstrate that the method significantly improves approximation accuracy compared to conventional fixed-parameter schemes, while requiring substantially fewer training resources than standard physics-informed neural networks. The results indicate that the proposed analytical-neural framework provides an efficient and flexible tool for parametric analysis and simulation of nonlinear dynamical systems.

title: Transition of the Electric Interlocking System Control Panel to Computer-Based Control
authors: Sunnatillo T. Boltayev, Bobomurod B. Rakhmonov, Charos M. Aripova
abstract: This article analyzes the practical condition of traditional control panels and display boards used in managing train movements at railway stations. Without introducing changes to the existing electric interlocking system, a method is proposed for organizing an automated workstation for the station duty officer through additional interfacing devices. Control and monitoring processes are implemented using a programmable logic controller, while data are visualized via a dedicated software interface. The system’s effectiveness is evaluated based on the speed of control operations, the reliability of data exchange, and the reduction of human factor influence. The obtained results demonstrate that the proposed solution contributes to improving traffic safety at stations and easing the workload of duty officers.

title: Optimizing the Calculation of the Word Error Rate: The Problem of Text Bias in Speech Recognition Systems
authors: A. P. Gavrikov, D. V. Beilekchi, A. A. Kolpakov
abstract: The article is devoted to the problem of text displacement in the classical algorithm for evaluating the accuracy of speech recognition systems – the Word Error Rate (WER). It is noted that the standard method based on the Levenshtein distance leads to a significant overestimation of the error rate in the presence of extra words in the recognized text, since one incorrect word causes a cascading bias when comparing subsequent words. To solve this problem, a modification of the WER algorithm is proposed, in which the elementary units of comparison are not characters, but whole words. The modified algorithm also uses the principles of distance editing, but operates with the operations of inserting, deleting and replacing words, which allows you to correctly take into account the semantic structure of the text and avoid the effect of cascading displacement. The effectiveness of the proposed approach has been confirmed by experimental studies using the Vosk and Whisper libraries on audio samples of varying complexity. The results showed that the modified algorithm produces significantly more objective WER values (10-15%) compared to the classical one (40-60%) in the presence of inserts in the recognized text. The practical significance of the work lies in the possibility of using advanced metrics for more accurate comparison and optimization of speech recognition systems in applications such as voice assistants and automatic transcription systems.

title: Dataset Generator for Organizing Research-Based Teaching Activities in Machine Learning while Studying Image Recognition
authors: Almaz Vildanov, Evgeny M. Devyatkin
abstract: The article presents a methodology for research-based teaching of students in the fundamentals of computer vision, aimed at developing practical skills in working with neural networks. The primary research objective is to form students' competencies in developing and training models for solving image classification problems using contemporary Python libraries such as TensorFlow and NumPy. To organize the students' research-based area, an algorithm for creating individual datasets is applied using the Data Generator program developed by the author, which allows generating a variety of data sets for machine learning tasks in the field of computer vision. The research-based learning methodology encompasses several stages, specifically, initial mastery of fundamental image recognition algorithms, individualized engagement with diverse neural network architectures, and performance analysis. In the course of completing research-based assignments, students develop and test neural networks of different complexity, beginning from fully connected models and culminating in convolutional architectures, which allows studying the effectiveness of different approaches in practice. Comparative analysis of image classification quality clearly shows students that convolutional neural networks demonstrate the best results, thereby confirming their effectiveness in computer vision tasks. The proposed approach enhances students' training in artificial intelligence, develops digital competencies and strengthens the link between theoretical knowledge and practical skills.

title: Methods and Models of Data Transmission for Ionizing Radiation Registration Systems
authors: A. I. Vlasov, E. S. Bezborodov, I. K. Naumov
abstract: This article is devoted to the development of an energy-efficient and reliable data transmission methodology for distributed dosimetry monitoring systems based on Internet of Things (IoT) technologies. A comparative analysis of modern wireless communication protocols, including Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, ZigBee, NB-IoT, Sigfox, and LoRa(WAN), is carried out from the standpoint of energy consumption, communication range, noise immunity, and scalability. It is substantiated that LoRa technology provides the optimal balance of characteristics for solving radiation monitoring problems. A prototype of an end device based on an STM32 microcontroller and an E22-900T22D-V2 LoRa module has been developed. The device interfaces with a standard USB dosimeter and ensures the transmission of measured data to a gateway. Field tests of the prototype in urban environments and open areas confirmed stable communication at distances up to 600 meters and over 3 kilometers, respectively, with a packet delivery rate above 95%. The results obtained demonstrate the fundamental possibility of building scalable and fail-safe radiation monitoring networks for nuclear energy facilities, medicine, and environmental monitoring.

title: Mobile Robotic Platform with Three-Wheeled Transmission Motion Control
authors: S. F. Jatsun, E. V. Saveleva, A. S. Yatsun
abstract: The creation and implementation of robotic self-propelled transport devices is an urgent task for various industries’ development. One of the important tasks in the creation of such devices is ensuring specified work autonomy by such systems, which implies their independent movement within the allotted area. Among various locomotion configurations, those employing two individually driven wheels actuated by separate motors stand out as particularly promising because they provide optimal maneuverability. Consequently, the subject of study herein is a controlled dynamic system- a three-wheel mobile robotic platform equipped with dual motor-driven propulsion units capable of executing both linear displacement and rotational movements. This work purpose is in developing control methods for a small-sized self-propelled robotic platform with a three-wheeled transmission, as well as a diagram of it control system. The proposed mobile robotic three-wheeled platform is highly maneuverable and reduces the labor intensity of work on loads local delivery. The platform movement trajectory organization scheme, as well as the automatic control system (ACS) block diagram and human-machine interaction feedback organization, are considered.

title: ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE-PROGRAMMABLE WIRELESS CONNECTIVITY: Challenges and Research Directions Toward Interactive and Immersive Industry
authors: Haris Gačanin
abstract: This vision article addresses the research challenges of integrating traditional signal processing with artificial intelligence (AI) to enable energy-efficient, programmable, and scalable wireless connectivity infrastructures. While prior studies have primarily focused on high-level concepts, such as the potential role of large language models (LLMs) in 6G systems, this article advances the discussion by emphasizing integration challenges and research opportunities at the system level. Specifically, this article examines the role of compact AI models, including tiny and real-time machine learning (ML), in enhancing wireless connectivity while adhering to strict constraints on computing resources, adaptability, and reliability. Application examples are provided to illustrate practical considerations, highlighting how AI-driven signal processing can support next-generation wireless networks. By combining classical signal processing with lightweight AI methods, this article outlines a pathway toward efficient and adaptive connectivity solutions for 6G and beyond.

title: Improved Nondestructive Ultrasound Molecular Imaging with Lightweight Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Jihye Baek, Dongwoon Hyun, Arutselvan Natarajan, Farbod Tabesh, Ramasamy Paulmurugan, Jeremy J. Dahl
abstract: Ultrasound molecular imaging (USMI) is an imaging approach that utilizes targeted microbubbles (MBs) to highlight biomarkers of disease. While differential targeted enhancement (DTE) is the current state-of-the-art for USMI, its reliance on destructive pulses hinders real-time clinical application. We have developed a neural network-based nondestructive USMI, validated in vivo using a transgenic mouse model of spontaneous breast cancer. To enhance training efficacy despite a limited animal number (N=14), we utilized several augmentation strategies including the use of several targeted MB types for each animal to generate independent image and texture patterns, alternative DTE approaches (sham and injection DTE), and random patch selection, overall resulting in a total of 15,350 patches to train the network. The resulting nondestructive USMI produces an image of the pixelwise MB classification score of the presence of targeted MBs. Our nondestructive USMI achieved a correlation coefficient of 0.954 with DTE, a continuous dice coefficient of 0.863 for a molecular signal coverage of the lesion over 20%, and a higher AUC than DTE ( 0.954 vs. 0.845 ) compared to the reference image developed from the contrast enhanced ultrasound (CEUS) image and manual lesion contour. Nondestructive imaging during continuous motion of the transducer under elevation sweeps yielded fewer artifacts and higher AUC than DTE ( 0.953 vs. 0.892 ), compared to the reference image. This demonstrates the potential of free-hand and real-time nonde-structive imaging. Overall, nondestructive imaging showed comparable performance to DTE under stationary conditions and superior performance to DTE under transducer motion, indicating its clinical imaging potential.

title: Coordinated Market Operations for Multi-Stakeholder Microgrids: A Local Sharing Framework Integrating Power, Carbon, Green Certificates, and Ancillary Services_supp1-3684943.pdf
authors: Longxi Li
abstract: Clustered microgrids, capable of hosting high-penetration of renewable generation, are increasingly viewed as building blocks of an intelligent, low-carbon Energy Internet. However, their collective potential is undermined when operating in isolation. Furthermore, segregated markets for power, carbon allowances, tradable green certificates, and ancillary services (P-C-T-A) fragment price signals, causing inefficient dispatch, value loss, and weak cross-stakeholder coordination. To coordinate multi-stakeholder microgrid clusters in a joint P-C-T-A market, a local sharing framework embedded with the carbon responsibility sharing concept is developed, formulated as a bilevel optimization that captures strategic interactions between self-interested microgrids and the joint market center, while ensuring carbon responsibility's fair allocation. At the lower-level market, P-C-T-A products are jointly cleared via a continuous double auction mechanism. To solve this complex equilibrium, the Multi-Agent Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient algorithm is employed to empower microgrids to learn optimal bidding strategies under incomplete information. Results from a case study of heterogeneous microgrids in Dalian, China, demonstrate that the proposed framework outperforms alternative market architectures and carbon responsibility allocation schemes, yielding minimum cost and emission reductions of 19.00% and 9.40%, respectively, while enhancing distributive fairness and coordination efficiency. The reliability and robustness of the framework are systematically validated via multidimensional tests targeting parameter sensitivity, structural validity, and algorithmic stability. This research provides a practical blueprint for the market design and engineering management of community- and park-level microgrids, while contributing a novel theoretical paradigm for coordinated governance in multi-stakeholder Energy Internet systems.

title: Theoretical Performance of Timescale Steering Algorithm Based on Kalman Filter with Adjustment
authors: Yiwei Wu, Long Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Timescale steering algorithms are vital for generating time references. Among them, the “Kalman filter (KF) with adjustment” timescale steering algorithm has been widely adopted. Previous studies have analyzed the experimental performances of these algorithms, but their theoretical performances remain unclear. To fill this gap, based on several key previous research achievements, the transfer functions of the “three state KF with adjustment” steering algorithm are derived when the KF is in a steady state, making this algorithm equivalent to a digital phase locked loop (DPLL). Based on the transfer functions of the equivalent DPLL, the frequency stability and time synchronization error performance of this algorithm can be theoretically determined for the steady state KF. The experimental results confirm the theoretical conclusions. These findings provide theoretical support for previously reported experimental phenomena and subsequent research, indicating that in the future, for a steady state KF, the frequency stability and time synchronization error performance of the algorithm can be theoretically determined for any given free running timescale and reference timescale with arbitrary noise characteristics, rather than relying solely on experimental results.

title: Intelligent Adaptive Algorithm for Boundary Detection of Dynamically Changing Regions in Video Sequences
authors: Maruf Tojiyev, Ibodilla Himmatov, Odilbek Chiniyev
abstract: The problem of detecting the boundaries of dynamically changing regions in video sequences, such as flames and smoke, plays a crucial role in safety and monitoring systems. Existing edge detection methods (Sobel, Canny, Prewitt) are often insensitive to brightness variations in real-time video frames, leading to discontinuous or distorted contours. To overcome these limitations, this study proposes an intelligent auto-adaptive differential approach. The method is based on evaluating gradient variations, reducing noise using a Gaussian filter, and automatically adjusting threshold parameters through the Least Mean Square (LMS) algorithm. For each video frame, the algorithm adaptively updates the upper (TH) and lower (TL) thresholds to generate a continuous contour map. As a result, the proposed approach enables more accurate and smoother detection of dynamic object boundaries while significantly reducing background interference. Experimental results confirm the applicability of the method for real-time video analysis and its potential use in intelligent fire monitoring systems.

title: A Single-Phase Integrated On-board Charger for Plug-in Dual-Motor EVs
authors: Gerald Brakoh, Ashraf Ali Khan, John E. Quaicoe, Hafiz Furqan Ahmed
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: This paper presents a simple integrated onboard charger (OBC) for electric vehicles (EVs) with a two-motor propulsion system, especially used in all-wheel drive (AWD). In order to ensure seamless integration of the charging system, the proposed charger does not use any additional components but reuses the two three-phase inverters that drive the two distinct Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSMs) to charge the EV battery. The first inverter is reconfigured into an active rectifier that ensures unity power factor is drawn from the grid and a stable DC-link voltage is obtained. The second inverter is reconfigured into a DC-DC converter to control the output voltage and current for charging the battery. There are no additional elements present; hence, this innovation not only reduces the cost of EVs but also reduces the overall weight. The charger utilizes the motor windings as grid harmonic filters that ensure a total harmonic distortion below 5%. A simple proportional-integral (PI) and proportional resonant (PR) control is implemented to achieve these objectives. Furthermore, the motors do not generate torque while charging, making it a suitable solution for AWD EVs. Simulation and experimental results are provided for a 1kW prototype to evaluate the performance of the proposed charger using a 110V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rms</sub>, 60Hz supply with an output voltage of 48V.

title: Real-Time Operational Optimization of Microgrid via Piecewise Linear Basis Function Based Galerkin Approximation
authors: Xizhen Xue, Yan Xu
abstract: This letter proposes a novel approximate dynamic programming method for real-time operational optimization of microgrids. The general idea is to directly approximate the value function via Galerkin theory through an analytical approach, thus eliminating the need for iterative training and enhancing the computation efficiency. More specifically, the piecewise linear basis function is designed for Galerkin approximation considering the influence of limit constraints. Therefore, it effectively fits the piecewise characteristic of actual value function for a high approximation accuracy, and thus the superior solution optimality. Simulation tests and comparisons with the state-of-the-art methods demonstrate the advantages of the proposed method.

title: Calibration of Phase Error for a Wideband Precision Power Analyzer Using the Calorimetric Phase Standard
authors: Miyuki Nakamura
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: A novel phase standard for calibrating phase error for power analyzers (PAs) based on calorimetry is proposed. Wideband calibration of phase error in PAs is crucial as it typically dominates measurement error of active power in the high frequency range (10 kHz–1 MHz). However, it is difficult to maintain low uncertainty up to 1 MHz when performing measurement under conventional electrical power standards. In the proposed calorimetric phase standard, sinusoidal AC power was applied to a low-loss capacitive load, and the resulting heat generation was measured by a newly designed calorimeter. The measured amount of heat was converted into the phase difference between the voltage and current, and the calculated phase was then compared with the phase measured by the PA under calibration. As calorimetry was used, phase uncertainty remained low with frequency, allowing achievement of phase error calibration with an uncertainty of 0.009° (0.14 mrad) (k=2) at 1 MHz.

title: CoVA-IL: Zero-Shot Imitation Learning via Contrastive Viewpoint Alignment on Object-Centric Representation_supp1-3686977.mp4
authors: Shuo Yang
abstract: Imitation learning provides an efficient paradigm for acquiring robotic manipulation skills, yet policies trained in a single environment often generalize poorly to unseen scenes. To address this challenge, we propose CoVA-IL, a zero-shot imitation learning framework that performs contrastive viewpoint alignment on object-centric representations, enabling direct policy deployment in novel environments without retraining. CoVA-IL uses the target object’s point cloud as the visual input and learns viewpoint-invariant latent representations through contrastive learning, thereby improving robustness to background changes, viewpoint variations, and cross-environment shifts. In addition, we incorporate multi-level point-cloud augmentation into 3D visuomotor imitation learning to improve data efficiency and reduce the number of demonstrations required for training. Real-world experiments show that CoVA-IL maintains average task success rates of 82.5% and 80% under substantial background and viewpoint changes, respectively, and multi-scene evaluations further validate its effectiveness for cross-environment deployment. Moreover, CoVA-IL can learn basic manipulation skills from a small number of real demonstrations, thereby reducing demonstration collection costs.

title: Fundamental Limitations on Neural Network Density Due to Memristor Variability with Temperature
authors: Nirmal Solanki, Jayatika Sakhuja, Razi P Muhammed, U. Ganguly, S. Lashkare
abstract: In-memory computing (IMC) utilizing resistive random-access memory (RRAM) crossbar array implements multiply-accumulate (MAC) operations for vector-matrix multiplication (VMM), improves computational efficiency. However, practical implementations face critical challenges, such as limited analog-to-digital converter (ADC) resolution and sensitivity due to array variability and temperature. To mitigate this, the current amplification of MAC outputs can be used, improving SM and tolerance to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{I}_{\text {var }}$</tex> and 'T' (Fig. 1c) [2]. title: An Adaptive Simultaneous Perturbation Stochastic Approximation for Dynamic Parameter Optimization in Ride-Sourcing Systems
authors: Ye Tao, Lu Hu, Jun Zhang, Qiujie Dai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Dynamic optimization of matching intervals, radii, and relocation thresholds plays a critical role in determining the operational efficiency and service quality of ride-sourcing systems. Given the complex interplay between ride-sourcing operations and traffic congestion, coupled with tight time budgets for system optimization, we adopted a validated simulator that ensures both accuracy and efficiency as a rapid evaluation module. On this basis, we further proposed a novel Adaptive Simultaneous Perturbation Stochastic Approximation (ASPSA) algorithm. The ASPSA algorithm is embedded within a rolling-horizon framework to enable dynamic decision-making. A key innovation of ASPSA lies in integrating a tailored Deep Q-Network (DQN) to adaptively tune the search step sizes, accelerating convergence. More importantly, we incorporate an experience replay buffer and an exponential smoothing technique for gradient estimates, enhancing algorithmic robustness. We benchmark the proposed ASPSA against standard SPSA, two variants of ASPSA, and the state-of-the-art Heteroscedastic and Evolutionary Bayesian Optimisation (HEBO) algorithm on a complex road network of Berlin, Germany. The results demonstrate that ASPSA achieves a 55.3–77.8% improvement in vehicle relocation failure rates and enhances solution stability by 25%–32% relative to the second-best algorithm. Furthermore, across all 30 independent trials on the high-dimensional multimodal Ackley function, ASPSA consistently converged within a very small error margin of the global optimum, whereas competing algorithms get trapped in poor solutions.

title: Differentiable Inverse Graphics for Zero-Shot Scene Reconstruction and Robot Grasping
authors: Octavio Arriaga, Proneet Sharma, Jichen Guo, Marc Otto, Siddhant Kadwe, Rebecca Adam
abstract: Operating effectively in novel real-world environments requires robotic systems to estimate and interact with previously unseen objects. Current state-of-the-art models address this challenge by using large amounts of training data and test-time samples to build black-box scene representations. In this work, we introduce a differentiable neuro-graphics model that combines neural foundation models with physics-based differentiable rendering to perform zero-shot scene reconstruction and robot grasping without relying on any additional 3D data or test-time samples. Our model solves a series of constrained optimization problems to estimate physically consistent scene parameters, such as meshes, lighting conditions, material properties, and 6D poses of previously unseen objects from a single RGBD image and bounding boxes. We evaluated our approach on standard model-free few-shot benchmarks and demonstrated that it outperforms existing algorithms for model-free few-shot pose estimation. Furthermore, we validated the accuracy of our scene reconstructions by applying our algorithm to a zero-shot grasping task. By enabling zero-shot, physically-consistent scene reconstruction and grasping without reliance on extensive datasets or test-time sampling, our approach offers a pathway toward more data efficient, interpretable and generalizable robot autonomy in novel environments. Code is available at oarriaga.com.

title: A Hybrid Covert Attention–Augmented Motor Imagery Paradigm for Brain–Computer Interfaces
authors: Beining Cao, Yixin Guo, Yutong Tian, Fuqun Cao, Ying Chen, Chin-Teng Lin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Motor imagery (MI) is widely used in brain–computer interfaces (BCIs) and has been applied in neurorehabilitation to support motor recovery in stroke patients. Although numerous decoding algorithms have been developed and achieve comparable performance, their effectiveness fundamentally depends on the availability of robust bio-markers elicited during MI. However, inexperienced users often evoke weak MI-related bio-markers, making reliable classification difficult even with advanced algorithms. Consequently, inaccurate classification lead to erroneous feedback to users, which further undermines the usability of MI. From the perspective of feature representation, augmenting MI with additional cognitive bio-markers can enrich neural features, improve feedback reliability, and facilitate more effective MI training and use. Therefore, a covert attention-augmented motor imagery (CAA-MI) paradigm is proposed as a hybrid BCI approach, which integrates covert spatial attention (CSA) with MI to introduce additional bio-markers and enrich the features used for classification. A transformer-based multi-branch EEG fusion network (TMEF-Net) is developed to fully leverage the diverse EEG features elicited by the proposed paradigm for decoding. Experiments involving 17 subjects demonstrate that CAA-MI consistently outperforms traditional MI (T-MI) in both intra-subject and inter-subject evaluations, particularly under short decoding windows. With a 3-s input, CAA-MI achieved intra-subject and inter-subject accuracies of 89% and 81%, outperforming T-MI (82% and 76%). Neurophysiological analysis further reveals that CAA-MI evokes broader activations across occipital–parietal and sensorimotor regions, providing more discriminative EEG features. These findings suggest that the proposed paradigm enables more robust neural responses and offers a promising strategy for neurorehabilitation-oriented BCIs.

title: PEEL: A Poisoning-Exposing Encoding Mechanism for Local Differential Privacy
authors: Lisha Shuai, Nan Zhang, Shaofeng Tan, Haokun Zhang, Rui Hou, Xiaolong Yang
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Local Differential Privacy (LDP) has emerged as a dominant privacy paradigm in the Internet of Things (IoT) due to its lightweight, decentralized and scalable nature. However, the indistinguishability guarantee inherent to LDP mechanisms inadvertently masks adversarial manipulation, rendering poisoned data difficult to differentiate from legitimate perturbations. Existing poisoning defense mechanisms either incur prohibitive overheads incompatible with resource-constrained IoT devices or rely on rigid domain priors. To address this, we propose PEEL, a Poisoning-Exposing Encoding mechanism for LDP, which reformulates poisoning exposure as a geometric structural verification problem within the encoding subspace. Departing from probabilistic statistical filtering, PEEL exploits the rank disparity between legitimate sparse perturbations and arbitrary adversarial injections. By projecting LDP outputs onto a low-rank structural support, the mechanism forces poisoning attempts to manifest as deterministic reconstruction residuals, thereby exposing structure-violating output and rule poisoning attacks. Theoretical analysis confirms that PEEL, as a post-randomization encoding layer, preserves the unbiasedness and statistical accuracy of the underlying LDP protocols, while deterministically exposing structure-violating output and rule poisoning attacks. Extensive evaluations across six protocols and two real-world datasets confirm that PEEL reduces client-side overheads by orders of magnitude compared to cryptographic baselines, while maintaining F1 scores exceeding 99.8% against structure-violating output and rule poisoning attacks.

title: A Multi-channel Architecture for Concurrent Safety and Non-safety V2I Communications
authors: Danladi Suleman, Rania Shibl, Mingzhong Wang, Keyvan Ansari
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Vehicles-to-Everything (V2X) communications enable real-time data exchange between vehicles and their surroundings, with the aim of improving traffic flow and enhancing road safety. Within this framework, Vehicles-to-Infrastructure (V2I) communication specifically facilitates interactions between vehicles and roadside infrastructure components. Given the diversity of communications present in V2I ecosystems and their differing data quality (DQ) requirements, the simultaneous dissemination of safety and non-safety V2I messages with these varying DQ requirements poses a significant challenge. The IEEE 1609.4 MAC offers a multi-channel operation to address this challenge. However, due to the limited 100ms time slot shared equally between the service channel (SCH) and control channel (CCH), supporting high-frequency safety awareness messages on CCH while managing large packet size non-safety messages results in performance degradation in a shared IEEE 802.11p radio environment. In this study, we present a multi-channel application-aware framework that extends traditional architectures by decoupling periodic safety-critical communications via direct access to CCH and data-intensive non-safety-critical communications powered by IoT application layer protocol (ALPs). Furthermore, alternative transport protocols (QUIC and SCTP) were evaluated as underlying transport for IoT ALPs against Legacy TCP and UDP. Latency, packet delivery ratio (PDR), throughput, inter-arrival time, and connection establishment time were measured in simulation studies. The results showed that ultra-low latency is achieved on the CCH, while QUIC transport-powered IoT ALPs efficiently manage data-intensive payload on SCH.

title: Fuzzy Membership Function Dependent Sampled-Data Learning based Consensus Control for Nonlinear MASs and Its Application to Autonomous Surface Ships
authors: Anto Anbarasu Yesudhas, Stephen Arockia Samy, Ju Hyeon Seong
abstract: This study focuses on investigating the sampled-data (SD) H∞ consensus control problem for nonlinear multi-agent systems (MASs) modeled via Takagi-Sugeno (T-S) fuzzy rules, in the presence of external disturbances and stochastic packet dropouts, while incorporating a machine learning algorithm (MLA) technique. To do this, a fuzzy SD controller (FSDC) with zero-order hold is designed under bounded aperiodic sampling for the considered MASs, where each agent utilizes only relative state information from its neighboring agents. In addition, this controller employs a MLA to solve the optimization problem associated with the selection of the data sampling period, thereby reducing resource consumption while ensuring efficient operation under limited system bandwidth constraints. Besides, an appropriate membership-function-dependent looped Lyapunov-Krasovskii functional (LKF) is employed to analyze the SD fuzzy system, and the resulting conditions are further transformed into a common Lyapunov framework for controller design. Next, based on the constructed LKF along with the inequalities, a set of necessary conditions for achieving asymptotic stability with H∞ performance is derived through linear matrix inequalities, and then control parameters can be obtained to guarantee the less conservative stabilization results of considered closed-loop systems.

title: VilaFi: Low-Cost Visual-Label-Assisted Device-free Localization and Tracking System Using Wi-Fi
authors: Haixiao Yang, Hong Wu, Yuefeng Zhai, Jiyuan Li, Songhao Yang
abstract: Wi-Fi-based device-free localization and tracking demonstrates advantages including strong privacy protection, low cost, and wide coverage. Current Wi-Fi device-free localization methods primarily fall into two categories: model-driven and data-driven approaches. Compared to model-driven methods, data-driven device-free localization based on Wi-Fi Channel State Information (CSI) offers easier deployment and better adaptability, yet remains constrained by the challenge of fingerprint collection. Conventional Wi-Fi device-free systems can only obtain discrete positional fingerprints, lacking dynamic and continuous fingerprint acquisition, which leads to limited localization and tracking accuracy. Compared to radar, LiDAR, and wearable sensors, cameras can acquire target positional information at lower costs. In this paper, we propose VilaFi, a Visual-label-driven device-free localization system using Wi-Fi. Our monocular camera-based framework delivers relatively precise and continuous positional labeling, enabling the acquisition of rich sequential Wi-Fi CSI fingerprints. We implement device-free target localization and tracking using a hybrid architecture deep neural network. The system achieves device-free tracking accuracies of approximately 0.16 m and 0.2 m in Line-of-Sight (LOS) and Non-Line-of-Sight (NLOS) environments covering 38.8 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and 78.6m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> areas respectively, outperforming state-of-the-art approaches.

title: Neural Collapse based Deep Supervised Federated Learning for Signal Detection in OFDM Systems
authors: Kaidi Xu, Shenglong Zhou, Geoffrey Ye Li
abstract: Future wireless networks are expected to be AI-empowered, making their performance highly dependent on the quality of training datasets. However, physical-layer entities often observe only partial wireless environments characterized by different power delay profiles. Federated learning is capable of addressing this limited observability, but often struggles with data heterogeneity. To tackle this challenge, we propose a neural collapse (NC) inspired deep supervised federated learning (NCDSFL) algorithm. Specifically, we first define an NC solution for the multi-binary classification problem and establish its optimality by revealing its connection to the global optimum. We then incorporate the deep supervision technique into deep neural networks and fix the weights at both the output layer and an auxiliary hidden layer using the derived NC solutions. When this strategy is applied in a federated learning setting, it encourages different clients to produce similar hidden features, thereby enabling the proposed algorithm to effectively address data heterogeneity and achieve fast convergence. Simulations for signal detection in OFDM systems confirm the NC phenomenon and demonstrate that NCDSFL outperforms several baselines in terms of convergence speed and accuracy.
